questions.q: What does 'Uit welk land kom jij?' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Uit welk land kom jij?' = 'What country are you from?' Don't confuse with 'Waar woon jij?' = 'Where do you live?'

questions.q: How do you say 'My name is [name]' in Dutch?
questions.explanation: 'Mijn naam is ___' = 'My name is ___'. You can also say 'Ik heet ___' — both are natural.
'wonen' = to live/reside. 'Ik woon' = I live. Different from 'Ik kom uit' (I come from).

questions.q: How do you say 'I speak a little Dutch'?
questions.explanation: 'een beetje' = a little. A very useful phrase for the first lesson!

options: Meeting someone for the first time
questions.explanation: 'Aangenaam!' = 'Pleased to meet you!' Said when being introduced to someone.
'wonen' = to live/reside. Ik woon, jij woont, hij woont, wij wonen. Not to be confused with 'leven' (to be alive).

questions.q: Which question do you ask to find out someone's name?
questions.explanation: 'Hoe heet jij?' = 'What is your name?' You can also say 'Wat is jouw naam?' — both are natural.

questions.q: How do you formally ask someone's name?
questions.explanation: 'uw' is the formal 'your'. Used with strangers, elders, or in professional settings.
